description: Comprehensive recruitment industry intelligence compiled from white papers, benchmark reports, and studies across 74 publishers in talent acquisition, recruitment marketing, and hiring

appcast_benchmark_2023.title: Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report 2023 (7th Annual)
key_findings: Labor supply rebounded in 2022: labor force expanded by 2.5 million from Dec 2021 to Dec 2022
CPA declined ~22% YoY despite tumultuous labor market — Dec 2022 CPA was $25.14 vs ~$32 prior year
CPC stabilized near $1.05 after dramatic 2021 increases; Q4 2022 averaged $1.07 vs $1.10 in Q4 2021
Apply rates trended strongly upward in 2022, reaching 4.41% (3-month moving average) — highest of the 2020s
Rising inflation (peak 9.1% YoY) and wage growth (4.2% annualized Q4 2022) pulled sidelined workers back
Prime-age labor force participation reached 82.4% by end of 2022
Pay transparency reduces CPC: jobs with pay info had $1.49 CPC vs $2.30 for those without
Mobile continues growing: 69.41% of clicks and 66.99% of applies came from mobile devices
Apply volume peaks midweek (Wednesday 18.17%, Tuesday 17.98%) while apply rates hold steady ~5.9% Mon-Fri
No single candidate source performs best for every job — diversification is critical
CPA is now driven by consistently high-demand occupations (Healthcare, Transportation, Education) rather than just skill/wage level
Insurance saw the largest apply rate increase (+1.84 percentage points YoY), while Manufacturing saw the largest decline (-3.84pp)
37% of workers not actively looking could be wooed away; 72% are approached with offers multiple times per year
40% of active job seekers use more than 11 channels to look for a job
40% of people would refuse a job after a negative recruitment experience
Shorter job titles get more clicks: 1-3 words = 3.41 avg clicks, 4-6 = 3.13, 13+ = 2.75
Job seekers' #1 priority: stable job with good work-life balance

job_seeker_preferences_2022.passive_candidates_pct: 37% not actively looking but could be wooed
job_seeker_preferences_2022.top_priority: Stable job with good work-life balance
job_seeker_preferences_2022.negative_experience_refusal: 40% would refuse job after negative recruitment experience
job_seeker_preferences_2022.channels_used_11_plus: 40%+ of active seekers use more than 11 channels
job_seeker_preferences_2022.top_passive_reach: 36% personal recommendation, 31% professional network, 25% social media ads, 25% job boards, 22% web ads
job_seeker_preferences_2022.top_active_reach: 55% company websites, 52% search engines, 52% professional network, 50% job sites, 49% friends
job_seeker_preferences_2022.top_deal_breakers: Financial compensation, work-life balance, work flexibility
job_seeker_preferences_2022.job_ad_must_haves: 38% salary range, 34% clear task description, 30% easy to read, 30% flexible work, 29% transparent process
job_seeker_preferences_2022.higher_salary_woo_passive: 52% could be wooed by higher salary/benefits
job_seeker_preferences_2022.career_mobility_woo_passive: 52% would move for more seniority/upward mobility

appcast_benchmark_2023.methodology: Analysis of 255 million job ad clicks and 12 million applies from nearly 1,200 U.S. employers in 2022. Duration-based postings excluded; only CPC/CPA pay-for-performance ads included. Job seeker preferences based on BCG/The Network global survey with 3,100+ U.S. respondents. CPH data from separate scope.

appcast_terminology_glossary.title: Guide to Recruitment and Programmatic Terminology
appcast_terminology_glossary.description: Comprehensive glossary of recruitment advertising and programmatic terminology from Appcast

employment_advertising.job_analysis: Process of studying a job to determine which activities and responsibilities it includes, and how those relate to other jobs
employment_advertising.job_description: Written statement that describes the scope, duties, tasks, responsibilities, and working environment of a particular job (JD)
employment_advertising.job_requisition: Document used to request a hire (Job Req)
employment_advertising.job_posting: An advertisement meant to attract job applicants
employment_advertising.job_title: Words that describe the responsibilities and/or level of a position, typically a few words at most
employment_advertising.keywords: Words or phrases that are used and searched on by job seekers and hiring organizations
employment_advertising.job_location: Place of work where an employee performs their job
employment_advertising.salary_hourly_rate: Financial compensation an employee is given for work performed
employment_advertising.depends_on_experience: Typically refers to the right of an employer to offer a salary commensurate with experience (DOE)
employment_advertising.pay_transparency: Practice of allowing a company's employee compensation rates to be visible to other people, either internally, externally or both, often to further equality
employment_advertising.retargeting: Form of advertising that allows for ongoing targeting of potential applicants, people a company has interacted with at least once before

best_practices.optimal_description_length: Between 300 and 1,000 words for maximum impact

appcast_terminology_glossary.notes: PDF is primarily image-based; only the Employment Advertising section (page 9) was text-extractable. Additional sections on programmatic terminology, recruitment metrics, and ad tech concepts likely exist in image form.

appcast_benchmark_2025.title: Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report 2025 (9th Annual)
key_findings: Labor market in near-perfect balance, shifting power from job seekers to employers amid the 'Great Stay'
Apply rates climbed in 2024, ending at 6.1%, a 35% increase from January to December
CPAs remained stable, rising by only 4.8% over the year
CPCs rose over 27% in 2024, likely due to harder-to-fill roles and job board pricing
Cost-per-hire reached $851 with a seasonal push towards year-end
Technology had the highest apply rate at 6.41%, followed by marketing & advertising at 6.31%
Every single U.S. state saw a lower CPA in 2024 than in 2023
'Sitting-down' occupations experience higher apply rates and CPAs compared to 'standing-up' jobs
Apply rate in Maine is just 4% vs Nevada at 6.5%, correlating with unemployment rates of 3.2% and 5.7%
First year to include search and social media benchmarks from 150+ employers, 1.7B impressions, 35M clicks

benchmarks.data_analyzed: 379 million job ad clicks, 30+ million applies, 1,300+ employers

appcast_benchmark_2025.methodology: Analysis of 379 million job ad clicks and over 30 million applies from 1,300+ U.S. employers in 2024

appcast_benchmark_2026.title: Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report 2026 (10th Annual)
key_findings: CPA and CPH rose sharply in 2025 despite a softer labor market — recruiting isn't getting cheaper
Cost drivers shifted beyond labor supply to job board pricing and programmatic media models
Apply rates improved to 5.19% in 2025, partly due to increased job-seeker volume
White-collar recession deepened: Tech apply rate hit 7.14% (highest), yet CPH doubled to $2,795
Frontline 'standing-up' roles, especially healthcare (3.22% apply rate), remained costly and hard to fill
Sun Belt states delivered stronger apply rates but Southeast CPA is rapidly rising (no longer a bargain)
First time tracking full candidate journey from click-to-hire with disposition data
Full funnel median costs: CPC $0.83 → CPA $19.20 → Screen $131 → Interview $275 → Offer $758 → Hire $1,053
International benchmarks added: Austria ($46.15 CPA) most expensive, India ($7.93) cheapest, UK $15.43
Salary transparency lifts apply rate: 5.03% vs 4.51% for non-transparent postings
Remote has lost its shine as a hiring differentiator — advantage has narrowed significantly
53% of applications arrive in first 10 days of posting; job ads have a limited shelf life
1-5 minute apply time yields 5.37% rate vs 3.09% for 16+ minutes — keep initial apply short
Monday has highest apply rate (5.01%); post early in the week for maximum impact
Mobile drives 63% of clicks and 71% of applies; standing-up roles are 69%+ mobile
Job titles of 4-6 words get highest apply rate (4.80%); 13+ words drops to 3.83%

benchmarks.cost_trend: Rising CPA and CPH despite softer labor market

appcast_benchmark_2026.methodology: Analysis of 302 million clicks, 27.4 million applications from nearly 1,200 U.S. employers. 1.4 billion impressions and 42 million clicks for search/social. New: candidate disposition tracking and global market data from 18 countries.

pandologic_pandoiq_2025.title: pandoIQ AI-Powered Job Advertising Platform Intelligence
key_findings: pandoIQ uses 48+ job attributes and 200+ billion historical job performance data points
Platform makes 7,000 micro-decisions per minute to optimize placements
Used by 125,000+ employers across 1,000+ job destinations
Over 5 billion campaigns contributing 199+ billion historical data points
NAVi algorithm predicts job ad performance before ad is campaigned
Case study: Domino's Pizza saw 472% increase in applicants and 533% decrease in cost

benchmarks.data_points: 200+ billion historical job performance data points

pandologic_pandoiq_2025.methodology: Proprietary platform data from pandoIQ programmatic job advertising campaigns

recruitics_talent_market_index_2025.title: Talent Market Index (Monthly Reports 2025)
key_findings: Hospitality hiring costs soared 225% YoY in January 2025
Transportation & Logistics recruitment costs rose 131.25% YoY in January 2025
May 2025: Broad-based declines in talent attraction prices across all job segments
June 2025: Sales hiring surged, likely tied to Q2 performance pushes
July 2025: Sales prices up 78.7% MoM, Hospitality +53.2%, Finance & Ops +33.3%
5,800+ U.S. stores shut in H1 2025, with forecasts projecting 15,000 closures by year-end
Federal workforce transition impacted private-sector hiring strategies
Index rooted in billions of data points spanning organizations of all sizes across diverse verticals

benchmarks.methodology_basis: Billions of data points from observed CPC and CPA metrics

recruitics_talent_market_index_2025.methodology: Rooted in billions of data points from observed advertising campaign metrics (CPC, CPA) across organizations of all sizes and diverse verticals

radancy_employer_brand_insights_2025.title: Employer Brand & Career Site Intelligence 2025-2026
key_findings: Companies with strong employer brands enjoy 50% lower cost per hire and 28% lower turnover (citing Universum data)
Only 1% of companies have fully matured in embedding AI into workflows
Leadership is moving too slowly to deploy AI capabilities at scale
Career sites using AI and ML to personalize content see improved engagement
TalentBrew platform tracks CPA, CPH, time to hire, hire ratio, quality of application and quality of hire
radancy_employer_brand_insights_2025.methodology: Radancy platform analytics data and third-party employer brand research

joveo_recruiting_benchmarks_2026.title: Recruiting Benchmarks Report 2026
key_findings: Up to 9x growth in applicant volume over the past three years for some roles
White-collar roles saw applicant floods while location-bound licensed roles remained supply-constrained
Volume increase did not translate into easier hiring; manual screening became the bottleneck
Tech postings asking for 5+ years of experience rose from 37% to 42%
Recruiters often hired from first wave of applicants, never reaching best-fit candidates deeper in queue
Structural constraints persist in jobs requiring licensed or credentialed talent
Launched 'Ask Joveo' AI agent for conversation-driven analytics

benchmarks.coverage: 16 major occupations across all U.S. states, millions of job postings and applications

joveo_recruiting_benchmarks_2026.methodology: Data from Joveo's Interactive Insights platform and JobGet, covering 16 major occupations across all U.S. states

key_findings: 97%+ of Talroo's frontline worker profiles are not active on major B2B social networking sites
Majority of Talroo's audience cannot be found on any other job site (per SimilarWeb data)
SmartQualify drove up to 80% hire-rate gains
Talroo Pro delivers 3x better performance compared to basic product
U.S. job market expected to grow 4% between 2023-2033, adding 6.7 million new jobs (BLS)
Won HR Tech Award for Best Frontline-Focused Solution in Talent Acquisition
talroo_frontline_hiring_2025.methodology: First-party platform data, SimilarWeb analysis, employer case studies

jobadx_programmatic_exchange_2025.publisher: JobAdX (now part of Shaker Recruitment Marketing)
jobadx_programmatic_exchange_2025.title: Programmatic Job Ad Exchange Performance Data
key_findings: Within first two months of using JobAdX, employers reduce CPA by 31%
Job advertising reach increases by 1,200% through the exchange
ROI increases by 45% within a few months of adoption
Now part of Shaker Recruitment Marketing as of 2025
RTB-enabled platform with dynamic bidding system adjusting placements based on real-time data

indeed_hiring_lab_2025_2026.title: Indeed Hiring Lab 2025-2026 Reports (Labor Market Trends, JOLTS Analysis, Workforce Insights Survey)
key_findings: Labor market in 'low hire, low fire' frozen landscape
Job Postings Index sliding from 10%+ above pre-pandemic to barely above norms
December 2025: 7.5M unemployed vs 6.5M job vacancies - nearly 1M more unemployed than openings
Openings-to-unemployment ratio at post-pandemic low of 0.9 (lowest since mid-2017 outside pandemic)
Healthcare accounted for ~75% of all net job growth in 2025
57.8% of Indeed job postings list pay information (up from 52.2% in 2023)
White-collar sectors (tech, media, professional services) significantly weaker, well below pre-pandemic
Software development postings 30%+ below pre-pandemic levels
Unemployment rose substantially for those without high school diploma: 6.0% to 6.8%
Blue Chip GDP growth forecast for 2026: 1.8% (range 0.9%-2.5%)
indeed_hiring_lab_2025_2026.methodology: Indeed's proprietary job postings data, supplemented by BLS data and worker surveys

key_findings: AI created 1.3 million new roles including AI Engineers, Forward-Deployed Engineers, Data Annotators
600,000+ new AI-enabled data centre jobs created
52% of people say they are job hunting in 2026, while ~80% feel unprepared
Hiring remains 20-35% below pre-pandemic levels in advanced economies
India (+40%) and UAE (+37%) showing continued hiring momentum
Nearly 60% of Gen Z view technical trades as more meaningful career options
Employees skilled at GAI are 5x more likely to develop creative ideation and emotional intelligence
Companies with more GAI-skilled employees see 4x higher leadership promotion and 5x overall promotion
Skills-based hiring: 22% more likely to receive InMail acceptances, 60% more likely to make successful hire
Head of AI positions surging across Australia, Canada, India, Germany, UK, and US

glassdoor_worklife_trends_2025.title: Worklife Trends 2025
key_findings: U.S. worker quit rate fell to 1.9% in September 2024, first time below 2% since June 2020
Career opportunities ratings fell to 3.67 from 3.79 in 2022, a 3% drop
IT saw -7.5%, pharmaceutical & biotech -7%, media & communication -5.8% drops in career ratings
39% of employees have a side hustle; 57% of Gen Z, 48% of Millennials
New business applications surged 47% from 293K/month in 2019 to 431K/month in 2024
Managers-to-individual-contributor transition yields 11% salary bump vs 7% for staying IC

glassdoor_worklife_trends_2026.title: Worklife Trends 2026
key_findings: 'Forever layoffs': Small layoffs (<50 people) rose from 38% of layoffs in 2015 to 51% in 2025
Mentions of 'misalignment' in reviews jumped 149% YoY, 'disconnect' +24%, 'distrust' +26%
Job applicants 12% less likely to reject offer in 2025 vs 2023
Hiring rates at 10-year low
Career opportunity ratings fell from 4.1 in 2020 to 3.5 in 2025 for remote/hybrid workers
AI large-scale changes not yet visible; many anecdotes but limited data expected in 2026
Wage growth on the upswing for young workers with <4 years experience

glassdoor_worklife_trends_2026.methodology: Glassdoor review data analysis, employer surveys, economic research

ziprecruiter_labor_market_2025_2026.title: ZipRecruiter Economic Research: The Great Freeze & 2026 Outlook
key_findings: 'Great Freeze': Employee turnover dropped from 177% in 2023 to 50% in 2025
U.S. added just 584,000 jobs in 2025, worst outside recession since 2003
Healthcare alone accounted for ~69% of all job growth in 2025
63% of businesses plan to increase hiring in 2026, especially entry-level
32% of employers plan to hire more entry-level workers over all other roles
44.2% of job seekers quit due to low pay in Q4 (up from 31.0% in Q3)
70.2% of job seekers discuss salary by end of first interview
88% of entry-level jobs omit degree requirements
~70% of recently hired workers have less than bachelor's degree
38% of employers now use skills assessments in hiring
57% of employers kept base pay unchanged in 2025; 61% plan to raise salaries in 2026

icims_workforce_reports_2025.title: iCIMS Workforce Reports (Monthly/Quarterly 2025-2026)
key_findings: December 2025: Job openings rose 6% MoM and 8% YoY
Manufacturing +2%, healthcare +8%, transportation +12% openings growth
Applications rose 7% YoY in December 2025
Internal applications up 8% YoY, showing strong internal mobility interest
White collar APO nearly double overall average at 34
Computer and mathematical occupations APO: 65, business and financial: 62
Healthcare support APO: 25, healthcare practitioners: 15 (least popular)
84% of TA leaders say improving recruiter efficiency through AI is top priority
87% concerned about legal compliance with AI, 66% about candidate trust, 65% about bias
EMEA: Openings +13% YoY, applications +11%, hiring only +1%

greenhouse_workforce_hiring_2025.title: 2025 Workforce & Hiring Report
key_findings: 72% of candidates report job applied for turned out different from what was offered (bait-and-switch)
66% of candidates feel intense pressure, only 7% believe market favors them
26% of candidates say AI has made it harder to stand out
Cost-per-hire approximately $10K per hire across organizations
Time-to-fill: Executives 3-6 months (sometimes 9-12), other roles 6-10 weeks
Onsite-to-offer ratio: 4:1 benchmark, some as low as 2:1
Recruiter quarterly targets: 8-12 business hires, 4-6 technology hires
Recommended team ratio: 3 Recruiters : 1 Sourcer : 1 Coordinator
38% decrease in time-to-fill for companies using structured hiring
greenhouse_workforce_hiring_2025.methodology: Greenhouse platform data, OPEN Conference benchmarks, employer surveys

shrm_recruiting_benchmarking_2025.title: 2025 Recruiting Executives Benchmarking Report
key_findings: Median time to fill: ~45 days for both executive and non-executive roles
Median cost per hire: $1,200 for non-executive, $10,625 for executive
Average cost per hire: ~$4,800 (up from previous years)
Tech/engineering roles average closer to $6,200 CPH
Retail/hospitality CPH much lower at ~$2,700
Every open position costs $4,000-$9,000 per month in lost productivity
Only 20% of organizations track quality of hire
Over half of organizations have recruiters managing ~20 requisitions each
Critical thinking is #1 required skill within next 5 years (71%)
Entry-level: 30-60 days; Mid-level: 31-60 days (44%); Senior: 40% need 90+ days
shrm_recruiting_benchmarking_2025.methodology: SHRM member surveys, benchmarking data from thousands of U.S. organizations

Job searches taking 30%+ longer on average
Tech and Finance/Insurance had highest resentment rates at 25% in 2024
CandE Winners had 38% higher NPS at research/attract stage (59 vs 40)
CandE Winners had 36% higher assessment fairness perception
Values were #1 marketing content consumed during research (46%)
110 companies participated in 2025 program, collecting 66,000+ candidate responses

benchmarks.values_content_importance: 46% of candidates cite as most important

talent_board_cande_2025.methodology: Survey of 66,000+ job candidates across 110 participating companies globally in 2025. Cumulative dataset spans 2,000+ employers and 1.9M job seekers since 2011.

josh_bersin_ta_revolution_2025.title: The Talent Acquisition Revolution: How AI Is Transforming Recruiting
key_findings: AI-enabled TA technology yielding 2-3x faster time to hire
Only 17% of applicants made it to interview stage

TA market is over $850 billion and growing at 13% per year
Only 20% of companies use skills insights for hiring
Only 9% have a user-friendly skills-based internal talent marketplace
30-40% of today's HR roles expected to be automated by AI agents
TA evolving from a 'stack' to an 'ecosystem' approach

deloitte_human_capital_trends_2025.title: 2025 Global Human Capital Trends: Turning Tensions into Triumphs
key_findings: 75% of workers hope for greater stability while 85% say organizations need more agile ways of working
Managers spend ~40% of time on problem-solving/admin, only 13% on developing people
36% of managers insufficiently prepared to be people managers
40% of managers report mental health decline after becoming managers
AI reshaping work: automating tasks, shrinking entry-level roles, accelerating change
'Stagility' concept: balancing stability and agility simultaneously

deloitte_human_capital_trends_2026.title: 2026 Global Human Capital Trends: From Tensions to Tipping Points
key_findings: One-third of workers experienced 15 major changes in the past year
66% of C-suite say traditional functions must change, only 7% making progress
7 in 10 business leaders say primary competitive strategy is to be fast and nimble
Culture debt: negative consequences of neglecting culture during AI transformation
Synergistic performance: measuring aggregate value of human-AI collaborative work
Organizations experiencing sustained strain, rising trust concerns, and cultural friction
deloitte_human_capital_trends_2026.methodology: Developed with Oxford Economics, surveying 9,000+ business and HR leaders across 89 countries

key_findings: 66% of U.S. workers say compensated fairly, but 72% concerned about cost-of-living outpacing salary

mercer_global_talent_trends_2026.methodology: Survey of ~12,000 C-suite executives, HR leaders, investors, and employees worldwide. 11th year of the study.

bls_jolts_2025.title: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) - December 2025
key_findings: Job openings: 6.5 million in December 2025
Job openings rate: 3.9% - lowest since 2017 outside pandemic
Hires: 5.3 million, separations: 5.3 million
Quits: 3.2 million (unchanged), layoffs: 1.8 million
Job openings fell 966,000 over the year
Professional and business services openings down 257,000
Retail trade openings down 195,000
Hires rate still depressed at levels similar to 2013
bls_jolts_2025.methodology: Monthly survey of approximately 21,000 U.S. business establishments by the Bureau of Labor Statistics

conference_board_2025_2026.title: Employment Trends Index, Help Wanted OnLine, Consumer Confidence 2025-2026
key_findings: Low-hire, low-fire dynamic persisted through end of 2025
February 2026 jobs report: 151,000 payroll gain but rising layoffs pushed unemployment to 4.1%
Consumer confidence Expectations Index rose 4.8 points to 72.0 in February 2026
15.7% of consumers expect more jobs available (up from 14.8% in January)
Net hiring concentrated in healthcare and social assistance
More cyclical industries show weakness suggesting downside labor market risks
Two rate cuts projected in H1 2026
conference_board_2025_2026.methodology: Help Wanted OnLine in collaboration with Lightcast; Employment Trends Index; Consumer Confidence Survey

sia_staffing_market_2025_2026.title: Global Staffing Market Estimates & Forecasts 2025-2030
key_findings: Global staffing market surpassed $650 billion in annual revenue in 2025
Growth slowed to 2-3% annually, reflecting structural shifts
Americas staffing market totaled $205 billion
SOW engagements now represent 39% of MSP spend
Typical employer composition: 71% permanent, 29% contingent (SOW, temp, contractor, vendor)
Asia Pacific strongest performing region with double-digit growth
Industry challenge: margin compression and commoditization
IT staffing momentum building, but AI may rewrite rules
sia_staffing_market_2025_2026.methodology: SIA proprietary market research, industry surveys, financial analysis of staffing companies globally

bullhorn_grid_2026.title: 2026 GRID Industry Trends Report (16th Annual)
key_findings: Top-performing staffing firms 4x more likely to leverage AI
Firms using AI at any stage 3.5-4.5x more likely to have grown revenue
78% of firms with 25%+ revenue growth use AI tools embedded in ATS
55% of firms saw KPIs increase 25%+ from AI screening alone
Firms seeing AI impact are 6x more likely to have increased revenue
Leaders equipped for AI adoption 40% more likely to achieve revenue growth
Top-performing firms 57% more likely to be in advanced digital transformation
45% expect economy to improve in 2026 (down from 73% last year)
AI now competitive necessity, not just efficiency experiment
bullhorn_grid_2026.methodology: Online survey of ~2,300 recruitment industry professionals globally (North America, UK/Ireland, Benelux, DACH, APAC), Nov-Dec 2025

asa_staffing_data_2025.title: ASA Staffing Employment & Sales Survey and Staffing Index 2025
key_findings: Q3 2025: ~2 million temp/contract workers per week, up 6,400 from Q2
Q3 year-to-year staffing employment gap narrowed to 7.5% (from 9.4% in Q2)
Q3 staffing sales: $28.1 billion, down 8.5% YoY
December 2025: Staffing employment 3.7% higher than same period in 2024
ASA Staffing Index reached 92 by year-end (baseline 100 in June 2006)
Average hourly earnings for temp help: $25.04 (Feb 2025)
Temp help services penetration rate: 1.58%
Industry showed significant recovery in H2 2025 after challenging H1
asa_staffing_data_2025.methodology: ASA member surveys, BLS data analysis, weekly staffing index tracking

adecco_global_workforce_2025.title: Global Workforce of the Future 2025 & Workforce Trends 2026 Report
key_findings: Great resignation is over, replaced by return to ambition with desire for security
61% of organizations struggle to transition workers into new internal roles
Only 33% investing in data to understand internal skills and capabilities
'Future-ready workers' make up ~33% of workforce (up from 11% prior year)
Job security and fair pay overtake personal fulfillment as top retention factors
Blue-collar workers report higher salary confidence than white-collar peers
Workers increasingly taking control of own development and skill investment
adecco_global_workforce_2025.methodology: Survey of 37,500 workers and 2,000 C-suite executives across 31 countries, conducted Jul-Aug 2025

jobvite_employ_2025.title: 2025 Job Seeker Nation Report
key_findings: 66% of job seekers report burnout
44% say it's easy to find a new job (down from 50%)
Confidence in landing role within 3 months has declined
86% say company culture is important in decision to apply
33% get culture information from employer's social media
Application fatigue is a key concern
Employ platform database: ~600 million candidate records
jobvite_employ_2025.methodology: Survey of job seekers March 25-28, 2025, plus proprietary data from 21,000 Employ customers (JazzHR, Lever, Jobvite)

adp_pay_insights_2025.title: ADP National Employment Report & Pay Insights 2025-2026
key_findings: June 2025: Private sector shed 33,000 jobs, annual pay up 4.4% YoY
Pay growth for job-stayers: 4.6-4.7% range since September 2024
Pay premium for switching jobs hit record low in February 2026
Job-changers pay growth slowed to 6.4% in January 2026
New hire pay rose from ~$18/hr to $19/hr, led by construction and financial sectors
Share of part-time workers ~45% in 2025-2026, up 6 points from 2019
Layoffs continue to be rare; reluctance to hire and replace departing workers led to losses
adp_pay_insights_2025.methodology: Aggregated and anonymized payroll data from 26+ million U.S. workers, 15M+ pay-change observations monthly

mckinsey_future_of_work_2025.title: Future of Work Research & Strategic Workforce Planning
key_findings: By 2030, ~10% of U.S. workforce may need to switch occupations
Europe could require up to 12 million occupational transitions (double pre-pandemic rate)
S&P 500 companies maximizing return on talent generate 300% more revenue per employee vs median
92% of companies plan to increase AI investments over next 3 years
Only 1% of leaders describe current AI deployment as 'mature'
Companies with leading digital/AI capabilities outperform by 2-6x in shareholder returns
45% cite need for more/different experience as greatest barrier to occupational switching
46% cite outdated skill sets and insufficient training as reason for skills gaps
mckinsey_future_of_work_2025.methodology: McKinsey Global Institute research, multiple global surveys and workforce analyses

mckinsey_diversity_series.title: Diversity Matters Even More (2023) - 4th Report in Series
key_findings: Gender diversity on executive teams: 39% increased likelihood of financial outperformance
One-fifth of executive team members are now women (33% higher than in 2020)
Eight in ten companies have at least one woman on executive team (up from two-thirds)
Leadership diversity associated with holistic growth, greater social impact, more satisfied workforces
2015 baseline: Top quartile gender diversity 15% more likely to outperform
2018: Gender diversity 21% more likely to outperform on profitability
2020: Gender diversity 25% more likely; ethnic diversity 36% more likely to outperform
mckinsey_diversity_series.methodology: Analysis of 1,000+ large companies across 15 countries, supplemented by social listening of employee sentiment (note: methodology has been challenged by academic research)

gartner_ta_magic_quadrant_2025.title: 2025 Magic Quadrant for Talent Acquisition (Recruiting) Suites
key_findings: First-ever Gartner Magic Quadrant for Talent Acquisition, published April 2, 2025
Leaders: SmartRecruiters, Oracle, Workday
Visionaries: Phenom, Darwinbox
Recruiting TAS market: 16.7% CAGR
Talent management tech spend: ~$9B in 2024, projected $11B in 2025
By 2027, nearly all recruiting tech vendors will embed AI capabilities
AI transforming nearly every part of recruiter role
Regrettable retention to emerge as primary productivity barrier
HR to turn one-third of recruiting capacity inward for internal mobility
gartner_ta_magic_quadrant_2025.methodology: Gartner Magic Quadrant evaluation of talent acquisition suite vendors based on ability to execute and completeness of vision

paradox_olivia_hiring_2025.title: Conversational AI in Recruiting: Olivia Platform Data
key_findings: Recruitment chatbot market: $2.03B in 2025, projected $5.41B by 2030 (21.6% CAGR)
Paradox schedules 32+ million interviews per year (~1 in 10 U.S. job interviews)
~40% of firms used AI chatbots for candidate communication in 2024
Olivia cuts time-to-hire in half, increases applicant conversion 5x
99.78% of candidates rate experience positively
Automates 90%+ of screening and scheduling
Chipotle: 75% faster hiring; GM: $2M saved annually; 7-Eleven: 40,000 hours saved weekly
Organizations using AI report 340% ROI within 18 months
31% faster hiring times and 50% quality of hire improvement
Working for 500+ companies worldwide
paradox_olivia_hiring_2025.methodology: Paradox platform data, enterprise case studies, third-party market research

hirevue_ai_hiring_guide_2025.title: 2025 Global Guide to AI in Hiring
key_findings: AI adoption among HR professionals surged from 58% in 2024 to 72% in 2025
AI confidence grew from 37% in 2024 to 51% in 2025
53% trust AI-powered hiring recommendations (as decision-support tool)
63% report greater productivity with AI
55% automating manual tasks, 52% improving business efficiency
41% of companies use skill assessments in hiring
57% of candidates believe AI would reduce racial/ethnic bias (+6% YoY)
66% of candidates still oppose AI making final hiring decisions
50% would apply to job where AI helps make decisions
HireVue: 70M+ video interviews, 200M+ chat engagements hosted to date
Time-to-hire reductions of 60-89% in enterprise implementations
hirevue_ai_hiring_guide_2025.methodology: Survey of 4,000+ HR leaders and employees worldwide

dice_tech_salary_2025.title: 2025 Dice Tech Salary Report (20th Anniversary)
key_findings: Average tech salary: $112,521 (+1.2% YoY)
AI-related professionals earn 17.7% more than non-AI peers
AI-related positions saw 200% surge in demand
Manufacturing tech salaries up 15.1% (highest sector growth)
Consulting, software, banking/finance average >$125,000
Education sector: -10.9% salary decline
47% of tech professionals actively job seeking (up from 29%)
Only 45% received salary boosts in 2024 (down from 55% in 2023)
Bonuses dropped from 39% to 31%; merit raises from 41% to 36%
20% affected by layoffs, particularly those 45+ in management
February 2026: AI skills in 58% of U.S. tech postings (up from 51% in Dec, +108% YoY)
Cloud Solutions Architect average: $157,978
dice_tech_salary_2025.methodology: Annual survey of tech professionals, platform posting data analysis. 20th year of reporting.

handshake_early_talent_2025_2026.title: Gen Z Hiring Trends & Campus to Career Reports 2025-2026
key_findings: Median salary for full-time jobs on Handshake: $67,500 in 2025 (+$2,500 from 2024)
77% of full-time jobs include salary information (up from 73% in 2024)
Remote work leveling off at ~5% of jobs (down from peak 7% in 2022)
Job postings on Handshake declined 15% YoY; applications per job up 30%
69% of students find in-person events easier for connections
62% find virtual events more convenient
Finance and accounting student interest increasing significantly
NACE: College Class of 2025 hiring expected to increase 7.3%
~Two-thirds of employers use skills-based hiring for entry-level
handshake_early_talent_2025_2026.methodology: Handshake platform data analysis across U.S. universities, NACE Job Outlook Survey

universum_employer_brand_2025.title: 2025 Most Attractive Employers Rankings & Employer Branding NOW
key_findings: 75% of students feel positive or curious about working for employer using AI
57% have NOT developed AI-related skills (disconnect)
AI gap more pronounced for women (lower AI preparedness)
73% of U.S. respondents identified engineers as most challenging to hire (up from 31% in 2024)
Over half of U.S. employers actively integrating AI tools into hiring (up from 19% in 2024)
U.S. AI adoption more than double global rate (25%)
DEI importance dropped from 77% to 54% of employer branding leaders
15% now say DEI is not important to recruitment policy
Growing gap between employer priorities (go-getters) and student self-identification (balance, purpose)
3,000 companies nominated across 40+ markets each year
universum_employer_brand_2025.methodology: CareerTest survey across 40+ markets globally, thousands of student respondents. 37 years of employer brand research.

great_place_to_work_2025.title: World's Best Workplaces 2025 & Trust Index Research
key_findings: Survey responses from 9+ million employees globally
Organizations see stock performance outperform market by 19%
Employees 38% more likely to plan to stay long term in high-trust cultures
87% more likely to say they deliver excellent customer service
Trust Index measures credibility, respect, fairness, pride, and belonging
30 years of employee survey data worldwide
great_place_to_work_2025.methodology: Trust Index survey of 60 statements on 5-point scale plus 2 open-ended questions, across 25+ million employees globally

cipd_labour_market_outlook_2025_2026.title: UK Labour Market Outlook Winter 2025/26
key_findings: 72% of UK employers believe National Living Wage rise will increase employment costs
Public sector net employment balance fell to -11 (more expect staff decreases)
Civil service entrants decreased 30%+ from March 2024 to March 2025
Retail (64%), arts/recreation (59%), hospitality (55%) NOT anticipating vacancy problems
Compulsory education (72%) and care/social work (62%) still facing recruitment challenges
AI use across labour market now rife; AI reducing opportunities for junior roles
HR profession grew 42% between 2011 and 2022 (4x general workforce growth)
77% of organizations link benefits to at least one objective
cipd_labour_market_outlook_2025_2026.methodology: Survey of 2,082 senior HR professionals and decision-makers in the UK, Dec 2025 - Jan 2026, weighted by organization size, sector, and industry

rally_recruitment_marketing_2025.title: 2025 Rally Awards & Recruitment Marketing Benchmarks
key_findings: 30 awards across 17 categories including 3 new: Best Careers Site, Best Tech Partner, Best Agency Partner
Record number of entries in 2025, surging AI-driven innovation
Employee-generated content became expected standard (not just standout tactic)
Concern about employer financial future rose from 18% to 26% (2023-2025, +44%)
Layoff/role elimination concern: 12% to 18% (+6 points)
Career advancement remains #1 reason candidates look for new role (52%)
November 2025: 1.1 unemployed workers per job opening (up from 0.7 in Nov 2023)
LinkedIn hiring rate still down 3.5% YoY in November 2025
Winning campaign example: Ingredion saw 650% growth in Indeed applications, 1,525% LinkedIn engagement growth
rally_recruitment_marketing_2025.methodology: Rally Awards submissions from 50,000+ professional community, BLS data, LinkedIn data, Glassdoor Employee Confidence Index

market_research_mercor_growth_2026.title: Mercor Growth Trajectory and Market Implications for Recruitment Technology
key_findings: Mercor reached $10B valuation in approximately 2 years -- fastest-growing company in rec-tech history
Growth trajectory: $250M valuation (2024) -> $2B (February 2025) -> $10B (October 2025)
$519M total funding; 30,000+ contractors paid $1.5M/day; targeting $500M ARR
Three 21-year-old founders became youngest self-made billionaires
Positioned at intersection of two massive markets: AI talent hiring and data labeling
Captured data labeling demand fleeing Scale AI after Meta's $14.3B investment (49% stake) and CEO departure
Expanding beyond tech into healthcare and legal -- potential disruption to traditional staffing
Acquired Sepal AI (February 2026) to expand AI capabilities
Market implication: AI-native recruiting companies can scale faster than traditional platforms

market_research_programmatic_ai_sourcing.title: The Programmatic + AI Sourcing Integration Gap: The Largest White Space in Recruitment Technology
key_findings: Market is bifurcated: programmatic platforms (Joveo, Appcast, PandoLogic) handle job distribution but NOT candidate intelligence
AI sourcing platforms (Juicebox, SeekOut, HireEZ) find candidates but do NOT handle media buying
NO single platform combines programmatic job advertising with AI-powered candidate sourcing/matching
This is identified as the #1 whitespace in the market
Juicebox has 800M+ profiles searchable in natural language; SeekOut has 1B+ profiles; HireEZ has 750M+ profiles
Joveo's distribution infrastructure (1,238+ publishers, 40+ countries) + candidate intelligence layer would be uniquely positioned
The company that bridges this gap will define the next generation of recruitment marketing
Skills-based matching combined with programmatic distribution could reduce wasted ad spend by 30-50%

market_research_post_hire_attribution_20.title: Post-Hire Attribution: The Holy Grail Metric in Recruitment Marketing
key_findings: Quality of hire is now the top success metric for 31% of hiring leaders, surpassing cost-per-hire and time-to-fill
Everyone tracks cost-per-click, cost-per-apply, cost-per-hire -- but nobody tracks quality-of-hire by advertising source
The missing question: which programmatic channel produces candidates who stay 12+ months and perform well?
Appcast released the first click-to-hire benchmark report in 2026 (302M clicks, 27M applications, 1,200 employers)
This data was previously unavailable at industry scale -- proving post-apply tracking is commercially viable
Solving this requires integration with ATS, HRIS, and payroll systems to close the attribution loop
Joveo's 30+ API integrations position it to pull data from multiple systems
Companies that can demonstrate quality-of-hire by channel will command premium pricing

market_research_mid_market_programmatic_.title: The Mid-Market Programmatic Gap: An Untapped Opportunity
key_findings: Current programmatic solutions (Appcast, PandoLogic, Recruitics) are enterprise-heavy and complex
SMBs and mid-market companies (500-5,000 employees) lack accessible, affordable programmatic job advertising
Only ~15% of employers use programmatic job advertising today -- massive growth runway
Manatal proved self-serve AI recruiting at $15-55/user/month is viable -- same could apply to programmatic
Mid-market companies represent a large addressable market with simpler needs but significant spend
Self-serve programmatic with transparent pricing could unlock the next wave of adoption
Key barriers: complexity of current platforms, opaque pricing, minimum spend requirements, long sales cycles

market_research_global_programmatic_non_.title: Global Programmatic for Non-English Markets: The Untapped Frontier
key_findings: Most programmatic platforms are US/English-centric
Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing recruitment technology market at 25% CAGR
India alone has the world's largest working-age population and massive recruitment advertising demand
Joveo already operates in 40+ countries -- most competitors cannot match this global footprint
Local publisher relationships and regulatory knowledge create defensible competitive moats
Multinational employers need a single platform for global job advertising -- few solutions exist
Huge untapped opportunity in APAC, LATAM, MEA, and Eastern Europe
Appcast newly launched in UK; StepStone focused on Europe; local players dominate individual markets
First-mover advantage in non-English programmatic recruitment is still available

market_research_staffing_agency_programm.title: Staffing Agency Programmatic: A $200B+ Industry Awaiting Automation
key_findings: US staffing industry is worth $200B+ -- staffing firms are among the highest-volume job advertisers
10,000+ staffing agencies use Bullhorn alone
Staffing firms using AI see 24% more placements, 28% more jobs filled, and 60% revenue growth (Bullhorn 2026 GRID Report)
Bullhorn acquired TargetRecruit (August 2025) expanding Salesforce ecosystem to 150,000 users
Staffing agencies manage hundreds of clients with thousands of open positions -- they need multi-tenant programmatic
Most current programmatic platforms are designed for direct employers, not staffing agencies
White-label programmatic platform for staffing agencies/RPOs is a high-demand opportunity
Revenue model: SaaS + percentage of media spend = highly scalable
Joveo's existing platform architecture supports multi-tenant use cases

market_research_skills_graph_job_adverti.title: Skills-Graph-Powered Job Advertising: The Future of Targeted Recruitment
key_findings: 70% of employers use skills-based hiring, but job advertising still targets by job title, keyword, and location
No programmatic platform targets candidates by verified skills, certifications, or demonstrated abilities
Skills-based assessment is 5x more predictive of job performance than resume review
Available skills taxonomies include O*NET, ESCO, and Lightcast
Leading skills intelligence platforms: Eightfold (1.6B+ profiles, 1.6M+ skills), Phenom (talent marketplace), Findem (Success Signals)
Concept: target 'registered nurses with ICU experience who have recently obtained CCRN certification' rather than just 'nurse + Dallas'
Skills-graph targeting could reduce wasted programmatic spend by targeting only qualified candidates
Integration with existing skills ontology platforms (Eightfold, Phenom, Findem) is more practical than building from scratch
Early mover advantage available -- no direct competitor exists in this space

cost_per_click.description: CPC benchmarks for recruitment advertising across platforms
cost_per_click.notes: CPC rising across most channels due to programmatic bidding, job board pricing changes, and harder-to-fill roles. Historical dip in 2022 was short-lived.

cost_per_application.appcast_2025_trend: Rising sharply despite softer labor market
cost_per_application.jobadx_cpa_reduction: 31% within first 2 months of using platform
cost_per_application.notes: CPA diverging: white-collar roles flooding with applicants while frontline/healthcare remain expensive. 2022 saw significant relief due to labor supply rebound.

cost_per_hire.notes: SHRM and Greenhouse figures include internal costs (recruiter time, tools, travel). Appcast figure is media spend only.

apply_rate.description: Apply rate benchmarks by occupation and platform
apply_rate.appcast_overall_2022: 4.41% (3-month moving average, end of 2022 — highest of the 2020s at that time)
apply_rate.notes: Apply rates correlate with unemployment rates by geography. White-collar apply rates rising significantly. 2022 marked the first meaningful upswing after pandemic stagnation.

applicants_per_opening.description: Number of applicants per job opening
applicants_per_opening.notes: Massive divergence: white-collar roles drowning in applicants while healthcare/frontline remain supply-constrained

cpa_by_industry.healthcare: Highest CPAs due to persistent shortages
cpa_by_industry.technology_white_collar: CPAs declining as applicant volume surges
cpa_by_industry.hospitality: Cost spikes (+225% in Jan 2025 per Recruitics)
cpa_by_industry.transportation_logistics: Cost spikes (+131% in Jan 2025 per Recruitics)
cpa_by_industry.retail: Lower CPAs due to larger candidate pools
cpa_by_industry.notes: Industry CPAs are increasingly bifurcated: white-collar flooding with applicants, frontline remaining expensive

ai_adoption_in_recruiting.description: AI adoption rates across recruiting industry
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.paradox_chatbot_adoption: 40% of firms used AI chatbots in 2024
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.bullhorn_staffing_firms: 3.5-4.5x more likely to grow revenue with AI
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.icims_ta_leaders_priority: 84% say AI efficiency is top priority
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.gartner_prediction: By 2027, nearly all vendors will embed AI
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.dice_ai_in_postings: 58% of U.S. tech postings mention AI (Jan 2026)
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.randstad_ai_agent_demand: +1,587% growth in AI Agent skill postings
ai_adoption_in_recruiting.josh_bersin_impact: 2-3x faster hiring, 60% application abandonment for slow processes

2026_update.companies_using_ai: 87% of companies now use AI in recruitment
2026_update.plan_to_increase: 93% of recruiters plan to increase AI usage in 2026
2026_update.staffing_ai_impact: 24% more placements for staffing firms using AI (Bullhorn 2026 GRID Report)
2026_update.quality_of_hire_metric: Now top success metric for 31% of hiring leaders
2026_update.agentic_ai_shift: 50%+ of talent leaders planning autonomous AI agents in 2026

candidate_experience_benchmarks.description: Candidate experience metrics across the industry

skills_and_workforce_trends.description: Skills gap and workforce evolution data
skills_and_workforce_trends.linkedin_skills_hiring_success: 60% more likely to make successful hire
skills_and_workforce_trends.korn_ferry_upskilling_retention: 67% would stay if offered, but only 30% deliver

remote_work_trends.notes: Remote/hybrid remains a powerful recruitment tool but availability declining

staffing_industry_metrics.staffing_firm_ai_adoption_benefit: Firms using AI 6x more likely to increase revenue

recruiting_technology_market.description: TA technology market size and growth
